zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Coker於大約1527年出生在英國多瑟特馬普德時，她的父親Robert Coker39歲，母親Elizabeth Beaumont27歲。她於大約1545年與Richard Watkins在英國英格蘭多瑟特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國多瑟特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Coker于大约1527年出生在英国多瑟特馬普德时，她的父亲Robert Coker39岁，母亲Elizabeth Beaumont27岁。她于大约1545年与Richard Watkins在英国英格兰多瑟特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国多瑟特。

nameMeanings.name: Coker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from a group of villages in Somerset named with Coker, from a Celtic river name meaning ‘crooked’.

variants.name: Cocker
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): nickname for a quarrelsome person, from Middle English coker ‘fighter, wrangler’.
English: occupational name, ‘one who puts up hay in cocks’, a seasonal occupation.
Probably an Americanized form of German   Kocher  .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Coker was born about 1527, in Mappowder, Dorset, England, her father, Robert Coker, was 39 and her mother, Elizabeth Beaumont, was 27. She married Richard Watkins about 1545, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Dorset, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Coker(이)가 대략 1527년 영국 도르세트 마포우더에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Coker(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Beaumont(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트에서 Richard Watkins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 도르세트에서 사망했다.
